channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nova Scotia Man Charged with Assault and Racially Offensive Language at Halifax Costco
item.description: A man in Nova Scotia has been charged with assault and using racially offensive language after an incident at a Costco in Halifax. The incident, which occurred in the east-end of the city, involved th...

item.title: Murder investigation into death of former UK minister Ann Widdecombe
item.description: British police have initiated a murder investigation into the death of Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former government minister and Conservative MP who served from 1987 to 2010. Widdecombe, who held j...

item.title: Kenya Introduces Land Reclamation Bill to Combat Degradation and Enhance Food Security
item.description: The Kenyan government has advanced plans to introduce the Land Reclamation Bill 2026, a legislative framework aimed at restoring degraded land and improving food and water security. Cabinet Secretary ...

item.title: Far-right Protester Interrupts PM Modi's Stay in Melbourne with Anti-Indian Remarks
item.description: A far-right anti-immigrant protester disrupted Prime Minister Narendra Modi's stay in Melbourne, Australia, by hurling abusive slogans from a hotel balcony. The incident occurred shortly after Modi's ...

item.title: Woman gives birth while asleep, nurses find newborn under blanket
item.description: A 27-year-old Canadian woman, Carolina Moreno, shared her shocking experience of giving birth while asleep during induced labour. She was admitted to hospital for a planned induction after testing pos...

item.title: Oratomic secures $300M to develop error-corrected quantum computer with 20K qubits
item.description: Oratomic, a quantum computing startup founded by Caltech physicists, has raised $300 million in a Series A round led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures. The funding aims to b...

item.title: Government heightens alert as monsoon rains pose nationwide flood risk
item.description: The federal government has ordered a high state of readiness amid increased risks of floods across Pakistan during the ongoing monsoon season. The Emergency Response Committee (ERC) meeting at NDMA he...

item.title: The Evolution of Football Tactics: From Simple Formations to Data-Driven Strategies
item.description: This article explores how football tactics have transformed from the early days of simple formations like 1-2-7 to today's complex, data-driven strategies. In the late 19th century, football was a bas...

item.title: Collingwood Coach Criticizes AFL Tech, Magpies Secure Tight Victory Over North Melbourne
item.description: Collingwood coach Craig McRae has publicly criticized the AFL’s use of video review technology during a crucial match against North Melbourne, claiming the system isn’t performing to its full potentia...

item.title: King Misuzulu apologises for remarks in leaked historical videos and says the royal couple has moved forward
item.description: Zulu King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has issued a public apology after historical video clips showing him making offensive remarks towards his wife, Queen Nomzamo Myeni, were widely shared on social media....

item.title: Tanzanian Police Arrest 130 in Saba Saba Protest Plot Investigation
item.description: Tanzanian police have arrested 130 individuals across the country on allegations of planning and encouraging acts intended to disrupt public order during the Saba Saba demonstrations. The suspects are...

item.title: Senator Robin Padilla Clarifies Search for Zoom Recording and Legal Validity of Online Evidence
item.description: Senator Robin Padilla addressed criticisms regarding his search for the 'raw file' of Vice President Sara Duterte's Zoom presser, clarifying that he referred to the original Zoom recording. He emphasi...

item.title: El-Rufai's Detention Sparks Debate on Justice and Politics
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing legal battle involving El-Rufai, a former political ally of President Tinubu who has become a key figure in the opposition. His continued detention, despite being cha...

item.title: Trump Proposes Fencing Plan to Restrict Public View of White House
item.description: President Donald Trump may be attempting to limit public access to the White House by planning new fencing around the building, according to a report by the Washington Post. The proposal, revealed thr...

item.title: Alberta's Separation Debate: Challenges of Online Discourse and Face-to-Face Conversations
item.description: The article explores the growing divide in Alberta's political landscape regarding potential separation from Canada, highlighting how online discussions have largely replaced face-to-face conversation...

item.title: Zaire Wade Faces Felony Charge Following Domestic Violence Allegations
item.description: Zaire Wade, the eldest son of NBA legend Dwyane Wade, has been charged with a felony following a domestic violence incident in Burbank, California. Police were called to a residence at 5:30 AM after r...

item.title: Scotland Considers Moratorium on Data Centers, Threatening UK’s AI Strategy
item.description: The Scottish government is poised to consider a moratorium on new datacenter projects, which could undermine the UK's AI strategy. The SNP national council passed a motion urging a freeze on all datac...

item.title: Former Deputy President Warns Against Ol Kalou Train Amid Electoral Tensions
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has raised concerns about a newly allocated train service in Ol Kalou, warning residents to avoid it due to safety risks. Speaking at his residence, Gachagua a...

item.title: SEC Investigator's Stock Holdings Raise Ethics Concerns Under Trump Administration
item.description: A new report reveals that an unnamed Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) investigator was allowed to retain stock in companies under investigation, raising significant ethical concerns. The waive...

item.title: Veteran Broadcaster and Former Senator Derryn Hinch Passes Away at 82
item.description: Derryn Hinch, a veteran broadcaster and former Australian senator known for his outspoken media career and political activism, has passed away at the age of 82. His long-standing career spanned newspa...
